app.title: Poli-Shuk Elections

index.pitch: Guess the polls, guess the result, climb the table — (not) prize-winning bets on the 2026 election.
index.stCarried: ⟳ Carried from week {n} — click to update
index.stCarriedUnknown: ⟳ Carried from a previous week — click to update
index.rule1: Each week you submit two guesses: how many seats each party wins in the actual election (the final bet), and what the coming week's poll average will be (the poll bet).
index.rule2: Each guess must total exactly 120 seats — a real Knesset, but under your control.
index.rule3: Bets lock on Friday at 12:00. Until the lock they are hidden from everyone; afterwards — everyone sees everything.
index.rule4: Forgot to update? Last week's bet carries forward automatically and keeps earning points.
index.rule5: A good poll bet is worth up to 30 points a week; the final bet is measured against the real results for every week it stood, and is worth up to 100 — because the real election is the point.
index.exampleIntro: You guess whole numbers, but the poll average comes out fractional — here is how it looks in practice:
index.exampleTotal: Total error: 10.7 seats, so the week's score is 19.3 points (out of a possible 30).
index.carriedBody: Didn't update before the lock? Your last bet automatically rolls into the next week, including adjustments for party mergers. It counts like any other bet — but it's worth editing when something changes.
rules.lead: Everything you need to play: what you guess, when, and how you earn points.
rules.aboutBody: A fantasy game for the 2026 Knesset elections. Each week you guess how many seats every party will get — both on election day and in next week's polls — and earn points for how close you were. No prizes, just pride and a leaderboard.
rules.betsIntro: Each week you fill in two separate guesses, each a split of 120 seats among the parties:
rules.finalBody: What the actual election result will be. This is the main event — the real election is the whole point, so it's worth the most points.
rules.pollBody: What the average of next week's published polls will be. This is the weekly fun — fast feedback on how well you're reading the map.
rules.seatsBody: Every guess must total exactly 120 seats — just like a real Knesset. The form won't let you submit any other sum.
rules.seatsThreshold: For each party you guess either 0, or 4 and up — never 1–3 seats, because below the electoral threshold a party doesn't enter the Knesset.
rules.cycleTitle: The week, the lock, and the reveal
rules.cycleBody: There's always exactly one week open for betting. Bets lock on Friday at 12:00 (Israel time). Until the lock every bet is hidden — nobody sees what you guessed, and you don't see anyone else's. At lock time everything is revealed to everyone, and your bet starts counting.
rules.carriedBody: Didn't update before the lock? No problem — your last bet automatically carries into the next week and keeps earning points. It's worth editing when something changes, but not required.
rules.scoreIntro: The basis is simple: we measure how many seats you were off in total (your "error"), and the smaller the error, the higher the score.
rules.scoreErr: Error = the sum of absolute differences between your guess and the actual outcome, across all parties. Being 2 seats too high on one party and 1 too low on another = an error of 3.
rules.scorePollFormula: Poll bet: up to 100 points a week — 100 minus your error (never below 0).
rules.scoreFinalFormula: Final bet: up to 150 points — 150 minus your error (never below 0). Worth 1.5× the poll bet because the real election is the point, and counted for every week the bet stood.
rules.exampleIntro: You guess whole numbers, but the poll average comes out fractional. Here's how the error is computed:
rules.exampleTotal: Total error: 10.7 seats ⟵ week score: 89.3 out of 100.
rules.lbBody: Your total is the sum of all your poll points and all your final points. Because a final bet counts for every week it stood, guessing right early simply banks more scored weeks — there's no artificial early-bird bonus.
rules.pollsBody: The week's average is computed from every poll published from one Friday to the next — a window that closes exactly when bets lock. Every approved poll in that window counts once, equally weighted, regardless of sample size.
rules.voidBody: If no poll is published in a given week (it happens around holidays and during the pre-election blackout) — that week's poll bets simply aren't scored. You don't get a 0, and you lose nothing.
login.lead: Sign in, pick a handle, start guessing. No passwords, promise.
login.or: or with a magic link by email
login.linkSent: 🎉 Link on its way! Check your inbox.
login.notConfigured: ⚠️ Sign-in will work once the server connection is configured.

login.onboardTitle: Wait, what should we call you?
login.onboardLead: Pick a handle for the leaderboard. It can't be changed later, so no pressure.
login.handleTaken: That handle is taken 😢 try another

polls.title: The Polls
polls.lead: All approved polls, freshest first. The weekly average counts every poll published from Friday to Friday.
polls.subThreshold: Below the electoral threshold — counted as 0 in the average
polls.empty: No approved polls yet. The robot is already on its way to Wikipedia.
lb.title: Leaderboard
lb.note: A final bet is worth roughly 2.5× a poll bet on average — full explanation in the rules.
lb.empty: No scores yet — the first bets lock on Friday.
archive.title: Weekly Archive
archive.lead: After the lock everything is public: the average, the bets, and the scores.
archive.notScored: ⚠️ Not scored — a week with no polls.
archive.empty: No locked weeks yet — all is revealed after Friday.
